title: Chinese Powers: A Critical Appreciation of Dingxin Zhao's The Confucian-Legalist State
abstract: Dingxin Zhao's The Confucian-Legalist State offers an elegant and clear overview of Chinese social development over more than two thousand years, identifying the ways in which the Chinese polity differs from those of Europe and explaining the dynamics that produced a civilian-led government that effectively marginalized military elites to an extent no other large-scale society achieved until the last couple of hundred years. This essay explains how Zhao's historical analysis both clarifies Chinese social developments over two thousand years and contributes to developing state theory.
